title: [LV] Separate runtime check cost from total overhead in profitability check
author: Mel-Chen
description:
In isOutsideLoopWorkProfitable function, there are two places where only the runtime check cost (RtC) should be used, but incorrectly included the costs of middle blocks and early-exit blocks.
1. VectorizeMemoryCheckThreshold comparison for interleaving-only
2. Minimum trip count that bounds runtime check overhead, i.e. MinTC2 calculation
This results in an overly conservative minimum profitable trip count.

This patch separates the runtime check cost from the total overhead cost, and uses only RtC for VectorizeMemoryCheckThreshold comparison and the MinTC2 calculation.

tests.test_body:
target triple = "aarch64-linux-gnu"

define i32 @int_select_with_extra_arith_payload(i64 %N, ptr readonly %A, ptr readonly %B, ptr noalias %C, i32 %threshold) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop ]
  %A.phi = phi i32 [ -1, %entry ], [ %select.A, %loop ]
  %A.addr = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 %iv
  %ld.A = load i32, ptr %A.addr, align 4
  %mul.A = mul i32 %ld.A, 13
  %B.addr = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %B, i64 %iv
  %ld.B = load i32, ptr %B.addr, align 4
  %mul.B = mul i32 %ld.B, 5
  %add = add i32 %mul.A, %mul.B
  %C.addr = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %C, i64 %iv
  store i32 %add, ptr %C.addr, align 4
  %select.cmp = icmp slt i32 %threshold, %ld.A
  %select.A = select i1 %select.cmp, i32 %ld.A, i32 %A.phi
  %iv.next = add nuw nsw i64 %iv, 1
  %exit.cmp = icmp eq i64 %iv.next, %N
  br i1 %exit.cmp, label %exit, label %loop

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret i32 %select.A
}

target triple = "aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu"

declare void @init_mem(ptr, i64)

define i64 @same_exit_block_pre_inc_use1_nosve() {
entry:
  %p1 = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  %p2 = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  call void @init_mem(ptr %p1, i64 1024)
  call void @init_mem(ptr %p2, i64 1024)
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop.inc, %entry
  %index = phi i64 [ %index.next, %loop.inc ], [ 3, %entry ]
  %index2 = phi i64 [ %index2.next, %loop.inc ], [ 15, %entry ]
  %arrayidx = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %p1, i64 %index
  %ld1 = load i8, ptr %arrayidx, align 1
  %arrayidx1 = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %p2, i64 %index
  %ld2 = load i8, ptr %arrayidx1, align 1
  %cmp3 = icmp eq i8 %ld1, %ld2
  br i1 %cmp3, label %loop.inc, label %loop.end

loop.inc:                                         ; preds = %loop
  %index.next = add i64 %index, 1
  %index2.next = add i64 %index2, 2
  %exitcond = icmp ne i64 %index.next, 67
  br i1 %exitcond, label %loop, label %loop.end

loop.end:                                         ; preds = %loop.inc, %loop
  %val1 = phi i64 [ %index, %loop ], [ 67, %loop.inc ]
  %val2 = phi i64 [ %index2, %loop ], [ 98, %loop.inc ]
  %retval = add i64 %val1, %val2
  ret i64 %retval
}

target triple = "aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu"

define i64 @vectorization_not_profitable_due_to_trunc(ptr dereferenceable(800) %src) {
entry:
  br label %loop.header

loop.header:                                      ; preds = %loop.latch, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop.latch ]
  %gep.src = getelementptr inbounds i64, ptr %src, i64 %iv
  %l = load i64, ptr %gep.src, align 1
  %t = trunc i64 %l to i1
  br i1 %t, label %exit.0, label %loop.latch, !prof !0

loop.latch:                                       ; preds = %loop.header
  %iv.next = add i64 %iv, 1
  %ec = icmp eq i64 %iv.next, 100
  br i1 %ec, label %exit.1, label %loop.header

exit.0:                                           ; preds = %loop.header
  %res = phi i64 [ %l, %loop.header ]
  ret i64 %res

exit.1:                                           ; preds = %loop.latch
  ret i64 0
}

!0 = !{!"branch_weights", i32 1, i32 1}

target datalayout = "e-m:o-i64:64-i128:128-n32:64-S128"

define i64 @pointer_induction_only(ptr %start, ptr %end) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %iv = phi ptr [ %start, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop ]
  %recur = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %recur.next, %loop ]
  %l = load i32, ptr %iv, align 1
  %recur.next = zext i32 %l to i64
  %iv.next = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %iv, i64 4
  %c = icmp eq ptr %iv, %end
  br i1 %c, label %exit, label %loop

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret i64 %recur
}

target triple = "aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu"

; Function Attrs: nofree norecurse noreturn nosync nounwind
define void @test_no_scalarization(ptr %a, ptr noalias %b, i32 %idx, i32 %n) #0 {
L.entry:
  br label %L.LoopBody

L.LoopBody:                                       ; preds = %L.LoopBody, %L.entry
  %indvar = phi i32 [ %indvar.next, %L.LoopBody ], [ %idx, %L.entry ]
  %indvar.next = add nsw i32 %indvar, 1
  %0 = getelementptr i64, ptr %a, i32 %indvar
  %1 = load double, ptr %0, align 8
  %gep.b = getelementptr i64, ptr %b, i32 %indvar
  store double %1, ptr %gep.b, align 8
  %2 = icmp slt i32 %indvar.next, %n
  br i1 %2, label %L.LoopBody, label %L.exit

L.exit:                                           ; preds = %L.LoopBody
  store i64 1, ptr %0, align 8
  ret void
}

attributes #0 = { nofree norecurse noreturn nosync nounwind "target-features"="+sve" }

target datalayout = "e-m:e-p270:32:32-p271:32:32-p272:64:64-i64:64-i128:128-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

define i32 @int_select_with_extra_arith_payload(i64 %N, ptr readonly %A, ptr readonly %B, ptr noalias %C, i32 %threshold) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop ]
  %A.phi = phi i32 [ -1, %entry ], [ %select.A, %loop ]
  %A.addr = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 %iv
  %ld.A = load i32, ptr %A.addr, align 4
  %mul.A = mul i32 %ld.A, 13
  %B.addr = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %B, i64 %iv
  %ld.B = load i32, ptr %B.addr, align 4
  %mul.B = mul i32 %ld.B, 5
  %add = add i32 %mul.A, %mul.B
  %C.addr = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %C, i64 %iv
  store i32 %add, ptr %C.addr, align 4
  %select.cmp = icmp slt i32 %threshold, %ld.A
  %select.A = select i1 %select.cmp, i32 %ld.A, i32 %A.phi
  %iv.next = add nuw nsw i64 %iv, 1
  %exit.cmp = icmp eq i64 %iv.next, %N
  br i1 %exit.cmp, label %exit, label %loop

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret i32 %select.A
}

target datalayout = "e-m:e-p270:32:32-p271:32:32-p272:64:64-i64:64-i128:128-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

define i32 @simple_csa_int_select(i64 %N, ptr %data, i32 %a) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop ]
  %data.phi = phi i32 [ -1, %entry ], [ %select.data, %loop ]
  %ld.addr = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %data, i64 %iv
  %ld = load i32, ptr %ld.addr, align 4
  %select.cmp = icmp slt i32 %a, %ld
  %select.data = select i1 %select.cmp, i32 %ld, i32 %data.phi
  %iv.next = add nuw nsw i64 %iv, 1
  %exit.cmp = icmp eq i64 %iv.next, %N
  br i1 %exit.cmp, label %exit, label %loop

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret i32 %select.data
}

target datalayout = "e-m:e-p270:32:32-p271:32:32-p272:64:64-i64:64-i128:128-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

define i8 @simple_csa_byte_select(i64 %N, ptr %data, i8 %a) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop ]
  %data.phi = phi i8 [ -1, %entry ], [ %select.data, %loop ]
  %ld.addr = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %data, i64 %iv
  %ld = load i8, ptr %ld.addr, align 4
  %select.cmp = icmp slt i8 %a, %ld
  %select.data = select i1 %select.cmp, i8 %ld, i8 %data.phi
  %iv.next = add nuw nsw i64 %iv, 1
  %exit.cmp = icmp eq i64 %iv.next, %N
  br i1 %exit.cmp, label %exit, label %loop

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret i8 %select.data
}

target triple = "arm64-apple-macosx15.0.0"

define ptr @std_find_caller(ptr noundef %first, ptr noundef %last) {
entry:
  %last.i64 = ptrtoint ptr %last to i64
  %first.i64 = ptrtoint ptr %first to i64
  %ptr.sub = sub i64 %last.i64, %first.i64
  call void @llvm.assume(i1 true) [ "align"(ptr %first, i64 2) ]
  call void @llvm.assume(i1 true) [ "align"(ptr %last, i64 2) ]
  call void @llvm.assume(i1 true) [ "dereferenceable"(ptr %first, i64 %ptr.sub) ]
  %call = call noundef ptr @std_find_generic_impl(ptr noundef nonnull %first, ptr noundef %last, i16 noundef signext 1)
  ret ptr %call
}

declare noundef ptr @std_find_generic_impl(ptr noundef, ptr noundef, i16 noundef)

; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nosync nounwind willreturn memory(inaccessiblemem: write)
declare void @llvm.assume(i1 noundef) #0

attributes #0 = { nocallback nofree nosync nounwind willreturn memory(inaccessiblemem: write) }

comments.body:

comments.author: alexey-bataev
comments.body: Can we also do something similar for the case, when the maximum trip count is known? Currently the value is hardcoded to be 16 iterations or higher, IIRC

comments.author: david-arm
comments.body:
> Yes I think this looks like a separate cost issue.

Yeah I'm looking into this at the moment. It seems like the getelementptr used by the store is being costed as zero, which is clearly wrong because we generate 4 of them - one for each store. There is also no cost attached to the creation of the predicated blocks, i.e. the extractelement of <4 x i1> and the branch. In VPReplicateRecipe::computeCost we bail out for stores if it's a replicated region and fall back on the legacy cost model, but that is woefully incorrect too.

comments.author: Mel-Chen
